content: The inbox uses JSONL (JSON Lines) format — each message is a separate JSON object on its own line, not one big JSON object. This allows O(1) appending and line-by-line streaming without loading the whole file.

provenance.raw_agent: Looking at the file again — it's actually JSONL (JSON Lines) format, meaning each message is a separate JSON object on its own line, not one big JSON object. Appending is O(1) and a processor can stream line-by-line without loading the whole file.
provenance.raw_user: Are all the messages in a single JSON object in a single file?
